channel.title: SSbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Meta, Microsoft look to trim workforces amid heavy AI spending
item.description: Meta told personnel in an internal memo on Thursday that it planned to cut 10% of workers.

item.title: The ‘Make America Healthy Again’ Movement Is Cooling on Trump and Republicans
item.description: Vaccine skeptics, ‘organic moms’ and environmental activists came together to elect President Trump. But some voters are disillusioned and might not t...

item.title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
item.description: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.

item.title: ‘Godmother of Silicon Valley’ Esther Wojcicki, mother of the YouTube and 23andMe CEOs, shares her secret to raising future leaders
item.description: Wojcicki takes inspiration from her daughters’ public failures and how they pivoted to turn mistakes into successful companies.

item.title: ‘It was like this little gift that fell in my lap’: Millions of Americans may soon discover they’ve been Canadian all along
item.description: If you have a grandparent, great-grandparent or even more distant ancestor from Canada, you could be Canadian.

item.title: Two Litigation Partners Depart Paul Weiss
item.description: The departure of Kannon Shanmugam and Masha Hansford is the latest in a string of litigators who have left the influential New York law firm.

item.title: People living in the U.S. with temporary protected status, targeted by Trump, are a $29 billion economic force
item.description: As the Supreme Court prepares to weigh the fate of TPS for Haitians and Syrians, a new report accounts for a demographic’s economic impact.

item.title: Saudis Withdraw Offer of Millions to Metropolitan Opera
item.description: The arrangement would have brought up to $200 million to the Metropolitan Opera, which has suffered a series of financial setbacks.

item.title: New Gene Therapy Enables Children With a Rare Form of Deafness to Hear
item.description: The treatment, the first of its kind, was approved by the Food and Drug Administration on Thursday. “Our baby was born deaf, and now he can hear,” sai...

item.title: Pope Leo says he carries a photo of a slain Muslim boy in his pocket: ‘I cannot be in favor of war’
item.description: Leo also suggested that the U.S. church is excessively focused on sex.

item.title: How the Supreme Court’s Shadow Docket Upended Climate Policy
item.description: The Times unearthed memos that signaled a major shift in the court’s operations, in a decision that critics say was rushed and flawed.

item.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
item.description: Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U.S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or retur...

item.title: Lebanese Journalist Killed in Israeli Strike Was Known for Being Intrepid
item.description: Mourners paid respects to Amal Khalil, who remained trapped under rubble for hours before emergency medics recovered her body.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump to Host Israeli and Lebanese Officials at White House
item.description: The preliminary peace talks, scheduled for later today, were moved to the White House from the State Department. The fighting in Lebanon between Israe...

item.title: Darrell Sheets, Auction Bidder Who Starred on ‘Storage Wars,’ Dies at 67
item.description: He appeared in 13 seasons of the long-running reality television show about storage-unit auctions. He later opened an antique shop in Arizona.
